# ICD-10 Code Overview

The International Classification of Diseases, 10th Revision, Clinical Modification
(ICD-10-CM) is a system used by physicians and other healthcare providers to classify
and code all diagnoses, symptoms and procedures recorded in conjunction with hospital
care in the United States. The system is maintained by the National Center for Health
Statistics (NCHS).

## Chapter I: Certain Infectious and Parasitic Diseases

This chapter covers infectious and parasitic diseases that are generally recognized as
communicable or transmissible. Conditions classified in this chapter include bacterial,
viral, fungal, and parasitic infections. The codes in this chapter provide information
about the causative organism and the site of infection where applicable.

Intestinal infectious diseases represent a major category within this chapter, encompassing
conditions caused by bacterial, viral, and other agents that primarily affect the gastrointestinal
tract. These conditions can range from mild self-limiting illnesses to life-threatening infections
requiring immediate medical intervention.

## Chapter II: Neoplasms

Neoplasms are classified in this chapter based on the behavior of the tumor (malignant,
in situ, benign, or uncertain behavior) and the site of origin. The classification also
distinguishes between primary and secondary (metastatic) malignant neoplasms.

Malignant neoplasms represent uncontrolled cell growth with the potential to invade
surrounding tissue and spread to distant sites through the blood or lymphatic system.
Early detection and accurate coding are essential for treatment planning and epidemiological
surveillance.

## ICD-10 Code Reference Table

| Code  | Description                              | Category                    |
|-------|------------------------------------------|-----------------------------|
| A00.0 | Cholera due to Vibrio cholerae           | Intestinal infectious diseases |
| A01.0 | Typhoid fever                            | Intestinal infectious diseases |
| A02.0 | Salmonella enteritis                     | Intestinal infectious diseases |
